title: Simple Wood Stool
summary: A four-legged stool with every leg splayed 5° in two directions at once — a small double-bevel angle that's easy to lose track of unless you mark each leg's inside corner before cutting anything else.
description:
The whole geometry of this stool rides on one small, easy-to-mix-up detail: the legs are cut with a 5° angle AND a 5° bevel on both ends at the same time — a true double compound cut, even though the angle itself is subtle. Stand the four legs up in their final position before cutting a single joint and mark the inside corner of each one. Every rung and rail in this build references those marks, and it's the step most likely to get skipped by someone eager to start cutting.

Build the two leg-and-rung frames as complete sub-assemblies first, then join them with the remaining rungs — the same sequence as a much larger table, just scaled down to stool height. Adding the additional rungs after the main frame is up is deliberately last, since they're bracing rather than primary structure, and gluing them (the plan is explicit — don't skip the glue here) is what keeps a stool this small from developing a wobble under someone standing on it.

The seat is either a piece of 1x12 cut square or a 12" plywood round, and it goes on from underneath — screw up through the top supports into the seat's underside, which hides every fastener from view on the one surface people actually look at while using the stool.

tools.note: The double compound 5° angle-and-bevel leg cuts.
Alternative to countersunk screws for the main joints.
Confirms the finished stool sits flat and level given the splayed legs.
Mark every leg's inside corner before cutting anything else — easy to lose track of on a subtle double-bevel angle.

materials.name: Self-tapping screws, 2-1/2" (for countersinking), or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"
Wood glue, wood filler, sandpaper, and finishing supplies

cutList.note: Ends cut at a 5° angle AND a 5° bevel simultaneously — mark each leg's inside corner before cutting.
Short point to short point, ends cut at a 5° angle, NOT parallel to each other.
Short point to short point, ends at 5°, NOT parallel.
Short point to short point, ends at 5°, NOT parallel.
Short point to short point, ends at 5°, NOT parallel.
Short point to short point, ends at 5°, NOT parallel.
Cut to a square matching the actual width of your 1x12, or use a 12" plywood round instead.

steps.title: Mark the legs
steps.body: Stand the four legs up in their final position and mark the inside corner of each one before cutting anything else. Because the leg-end angle is only 5° and is a double bevel, it's genuinely easy to lose track of which corner is which — always remember the marked corners face the inside of the leg.

steps.title: Build the two leg-and-rung sets
steps.body: Build two identical leg frames, joining the short rungs and one pair of top supports to each pair of legs, referencing the marks from the first step.
materials: Self-tapping screws, 2-1/2" (for countersinking), or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"
Wood glue, wood filler, sandpaper, and finishing supplies

steps.title: Add the additional rungs
steps.body: Add the additional rungs to each leg frame. Don't forget the glue on these — they're bracing rather than primary structure, and the glue is what keeps a stool this size from developing a wobble under real weight.
materials: Wood glue, wood filler, sandpaper, and finishing supplies

steps.title: Join the two frames
steps.body: Attach the remaining top supports to join the two leg frames into the complete stool base.
materials: Self-tapping screws, 2-1/2" (for countersinking), or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Attach the seat and finish
steps.body:
Attach the seat by screwing up through the top supports into its underside — this hides every screw hole from the surface people actually see and sit on.

Fill any exposed holes, sand thoroughly, and finish.
materials: Self-tapping screws, 2-1/2" (for countersinking), or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"
Wood glue, wood filler, sandpaper, and finishing supplies

images.alt: Small wooden stool with splayed legs and a square seat
